---
title: "Program Evaluation Standards and Theory Development"
description: "Study note on Program Evaluation Standards and Theory Development in Evaluation Theory, Models, and Applications."
book_id: "evaluation-theory-models-applications"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Major section"
source_pages: "101"
citation_key: "stufflebeam_coryn_2014_evaluation_theory_models_applications"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:evaluation-theory-models-applications:program-evaluation-standards-and-theory-development:c0005"
source_status: "needs_review"
---
## Source

<div class="chapter-meta"><span>2014</span><span>Source pages 101</span><span>Major section</span></div>
This note summarises the MCP research-library section "Program Evaluation Standards and Theory Development" from Daniel L. Stufflebeam and Chris L. S. Coryn's *Evaluation Theory, Models, and Applications* [@stufflebeam_coryn_2014_evaluation_theory_models_applications]. It is a paraphrased study note based on source-linked retrieval records, not a substitute for the source text.

## Core Argument

The section assesses evaluation theory as a field rich in concepts, standards, and approaches but still developing stronger validated theory.

## Study Summary

The section assesses evaluation theory as a field rich in concepts, standards, and approaches but still developing stronger validated theory.

It argues for converting useful approaches into better-tested theories through research and metaevaluation.

The practical implication is humility: models should be used critically and improved through evidence.

## Key Concepts

- Theory development
- Evaluation standards
- Metaevaluation
- Validated theory

## Source Location

- Section path: Part One: Fundamentals of Evaluation > Chapter 2: Evaluation Theory > Program Evaluation Standards and Theory Development

## Practical Implications

- Treat models as aids to judgment, not recipes.
- Use standards to critique an evaluation approach before adopting it.
- Document what the evaluation model predicts and how that prediction is checked.

## Concept Map

::: {.concept-map}
```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
  Problem["Problem: How does Program Evaluation Standards and Theory Development guide evaluation practice?"]
  Central["Concept: Program Evaluation Standards and Theory Development"]
  Concept1["Concept: Theory development"]
  Concept2["Concept: Evaluation standards"]
  Concept3["Concept: Metaevaluation"]
  Evidence["Evidence: Source pages 101"]
  Implication1["Policy implication: Treat models as aids to judgment, not recipes."]
  Implication2["Policy implication: Use standards to critique an evaluation approach before adopting it."]
  Question["Open question: What would need checking before applying this guidance?"]

  Problem --> Central
  Central --> Concept1
  Central --> Concept2
  Central --> Concept3
  Central --> Evidence
  Concept1 --> Implication1
  Concept2 --> Implication2
  Implication1 --> Question

  classDef problem fill:#fee2e2,stroke:#b91c1c,color:#7f1d1d;
  classDef concept fill:#e6f3f1,stroke:#115e59,color:#134e4a;
  classDef mechanism fill:#e8f1ff,stroke:#1d4ed8,color:#1e3a8a;
  classDef evidence fill:#fff2df,stroke:#b45309,color:#7c2d12;
  classDef implication fill:#fce7f3,stroke:#be185d,color:#831843;
  class Problem problem;
  class Central concept;
  class Evidence evidence;
  class Concept1,Concept2,Concept3 concept;
  class Implication1,Implication2,Question implication;
```
:::

## Connections

- Book overview: [Overview](index.qmd)
- Concepts: [Core Concepts](concepts.qmd)
- Map: [Chapter Map](chapter-map.qmd)
- Practice synthesis: [Practice Implications](practice-implications.qmd)

## Study Prompts

1. What is the main evaluation problem that Program Evaluation Standards and Theory Development helps diagnose?
2. Which assumptions would need evidence before using this guidance in a live evaluation?
3. How would this change the way an evaluator scopes questions, methods, or reporting?
4. Where could the guidance be misused if treated as a fixed template?

## Source Chunks

<details class="source-chunks">
<summary>Source chunks</summary>
<ul class="source-chunk-list">
<li><code>chunk:evaluation-theory-models-applications:program-evaluation-standards-and-theory-development:c0005</code></li>
</ul>
</details>

## References
